cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for ITAdmin platform operations versus academic deny-by-default
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.3 security and authorization tests and AC-23 security/scope gate apply.
Role-matrix fixture provides ITAdmin without explicit academic access grant and AcademicAdmin as control.
Platform health endpoint, technical audit logs, and academic resources (session roster, report, enrollment import) exist in test environment.
steps: As ITAdmin, call GET /v1/health and GET /v1/me to verify platform operations identity context.
As ITAdmin, call GET /v1/audit-logs filtered to technical action types (login_lock, system_config, infrastructure).
As ITAdmin, attempt academic routes: GET /v1/reports/attendance, POST /v1/enrollments/import, GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance, PATCH attendance correction.
As ITAdmin, attempt POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and POST /v1/check-ins.
As AcademicAdmin control, verify allowed academic routes succeed within institution scope.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §7.3 and AC-23 six-role matrix: ITAdmin succeeds on platform operations (health, technical audit, SystemOps) and is denied on academic mutations and bulk attendance reads without explicit grant per permissions matrix §3.1–3.2 and §5.5.

cases.title: M01 SystemOps and user role provisioning enforce ITAdmin technical boundary at DB layer
preconditions: Integration database seeded with users, user_role_assignments, and attendance records.
M01 Identity and Access module authorize(action, resource, scopeContext) and user provisioning service use real DB transactions.
ITAdmin actor exists without academic scope grant; target Student user exists for provisioning test.
steps: Invoke M01 authorize for ITAdmin with SystemOps.execute; assert Allow.
Invoke M01 provisionUserRole or equivalent provisioning command to assign Lecturer role to existing user with scope; commit transaction.
Query user_role_assignments and audit_logs for provisioning event.
Invoke M01 authorize for ITAdmin with AttendanceRecord.update; assert Deny.
Attempt attendance correction service call as ITAdmin and confirm no attendance_records mutation.
cases.expected: M01 module boundary permits SystemOps.execute and user account provisioning integration for ITAdmin; AttendanceRecord.update is denied at authorize; provisioning writes user_role_assignments with audit trail; no attendance_records rows are mutated per FR-33 and permissions matrix §3.2.

cases.title: HTTP contract for ITAdmin POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me returns technical operations role claims
preconditions: Provisioned ITAdmin account with institution-issued credentials exists.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with ITAdmin email and password.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.accessToken, data.expiresInSeconds, and data.roles containing ITAdmin.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken from login response.
Assert HTTP 200 with actor identity, ITAdmin role, and technical scope claims (no academic facultyId or classSectionId grants unless explicitly provisioned).
Verify response envelope matches standard { data, meta, error } shape with error null.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and ITAdmin role per FR-36; GET /v1/me returns HTTP 200 with ITAdmin identity and technical scope context required for platform operations per FR-33 and API §4.1.

cases.title: HTTP contract for ITAdmin GET /v1/health returns system health status envelope
preconditions: API service is running with postgres and redis dependencies healthy.
ITAdmin is authenticated with valid bearer token.
steps: Send GET /v1/health with ITAdmin Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 200 and standard response envelope.
Assert data.status is ok and dependency indicators (db, cache) report connected or healthy state.
Verify meta.requestId and meta.timestamp are present.
Send GET /v1/health without Authorization and confirm health remains accessible for operational monitoring per platform reliability requirements.
cases.expected: GET /v1/health returns HTTP 200 with documented health payload including service and dependency status per FR-33 system health views and NFR-16 operational telemetry alignment.

cases.title: HTTP contract for ITAdmin GET /v1/audit-logs returns technical operational log envelope
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated with bearer token.
Fixture includes technical audit entries (login_lock, system_config, infrastructure) and academic attendance mutation entries.
Known actionType values exist for technical and academic event filters.
steps: Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=login_lock&page=1&pageSize=25 with ITAdmin Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 200 and standard response envelope with data.items array and meta.pagination.
Validate technical audit items include actorUserId, occurredAt, actionType, and targetType without academic PII beyond operational context.
Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=attendance_status_mutation as ITAdmin without academic grant.
Inspect response for technical-scope filtering or HTTP 403 without academic mutation payload leakage.
cases.expected: Technical operational logs return HTTP 200 with paginated audit entries per API §4.7; academic mutation queries are filtered or denied for ITAdmin without explicit grant per FR-33 operational logs scope and permissions matrix AuditLog.read (technical audit only).

cases.title: HTTP contract for ITAdmin POST /v1/enrollments/import denied before row processing
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without academic enrollment import grant.
Class section with known classSectionId exists.
Import payload contains valid and invalid student rows.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import for classSectionId with mixed valid and invalid rows as ITAdmin.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope before row-level processing begins.
Verify no enrollments are persisted and no row-level acceptance or rejection summary is returned.
Query enrollments table for classSectionId and confirm zero new rows from ITAdmin attempt.
As AcademicAdmin control, send same import and verify HTTP 200 with row-level rejection assertions for invalid rows per FR-04.
cases.expected: ITAdmin enrollment import is denied at authorization boundary with HTTP 403; no row-level processing or enrollment mutations occur; AcademicAdmin control demonstrates FR-04 row-level rejection behavior separately.

cases.title: HTTP contract for ITAdmin GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance denied without academic grant
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
Open class session exists with mixed Present, Late, Pending, and rejected attempt outcomes.
sessionId for in-scope session is known; Lecturer assigned to section is available as authorized control.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with ITAdmin Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope.
Verify response data is null and no partial roster rows (studentCode, attendanceStatus, latestAttemptOutcome) are disclosed.
As assigned Lecturer control, send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and validate HTTP 200 with documented live roster payload shape per API §4.5.
Confirm ITAdmin denial does not alter session state or attendance records.
cases.expected: ITAdmin live roster request returns HTTP 403 without partial data disclosure per FR-33 academic edits out of scope; Lecturer control returns HTTP 200 with FR-19 roster payload shape.

cases.title: Primary IT Admin platform operations workflow: authenticate, verify health, review technical audit logs
preconditions: ITAdmin is provisioned with institution-issued credentials.
Platform is running with healthy dependencies and recent technical audit events (login throttles, config changes).
No explicit academic access grant is assigned to ITAdmin actor.
steps: ITAdmin completes POST /v1/auth/login and GET /v1/me to establish authenticated session.
ITAdmin calls GET /v1/health and records service status and dependency health indicators.
ITAdmin queries GET /v1/audit-logs with technical actionType and date range filters for operational triage.
ITAdmin attempts GET /v1/reports/attendance and PATCH attendance correction to confirm academic boundary.
Verify all platform operations succeed and all academic mutations are denied without data leakage.
cases.expected: End-to-end IT Admin happy path per FR-33: technical configuration visibility (health), operational log review, and identity context succeed; academic attendance edits and reports remain denied per permissions matrix §5.5 and AC-23.

cases.title: Alternate path: ITAdmin admin-assisted account recovery and role provisioning integration
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated with SystemOps.execute permission.
Student account exists in locked or throttled state after repeated failed logins per FR-36 security policy.
New Lecturer user record exists without role assignment.
steps: ITAdmin queries GET /v1/audit-logs for login_lock or authentication_failure events for the locked student account.
ITAdmin executes admin-assisted account unlock or password reset via provisioning integration command (MVP: admin-assisted reset acceptable per FR-36).
ITAdmin provisions Lecturer role assignment for new user via user account provisioning integration.
Verify student can POST /v1/auth/login successfully after unlock.
Query audit_logs for provisioning and account recovery events with ITAdmin as actor.
cases.expected: Alternate IT Admin workflow completes user account provisioning integration and admin-assisted recovery per FR-33 and FR-36; all privileged actions produce audit entries with ITAdmin actor; no attendance record mutations occur.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification: ITAdmin technical configuration change is recorded in operational logs
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated with SystemOps.execute permission.
Login throttle or rate-limit configuration is modifiable by ITAdmin.
Baseline technical audit log count for system_config actionType is captured.
steps: ITAdmin applies technical configuration change (e.g., login throttle threshold or session timeout) via SystemOps command.
ITAdmin queries GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=system_config&actorUserId={itAdminId}.
Verify audit entry records ITAdmin actor, occurredAt, targetType, configuration key, and old/new values.
Attempt PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} as ITAdmin and confirm denial.
Re-query attendance_records to confirm no ITAdmin-initiated academic mutation.
cases.expected: Technical configuration change produces immutable audit evidence visible to ITAdmin in operational logs per FR-33 and NFR-16; ITAdmin cannot mutate attendance records; audit trail correlates config change actor and timestamp.

cases.title: ITAdmin technical audit log pagination invariants at module boundary
preconditions: Integration database contains more than 50 technical audit_logs rows (login_lock, system_config, infrastructure) and additional academic mutation rows.
M08 queryAuditLogs supports page and pageSize with ITAdmin technical-scope filter injection.
steps: Query page 1 with pageSize=25 as ITAdmin and record meta.pagination.totalItems, totalPages, and item IDs.
Query page 2 with same technical filters and pageSize=25.
Query last page using totalPages from first response.
Verify returned items are limited to technical action types and contain no academic attendance mutation payloads without grant.
Confirm union of all page item IDs is disjoint across pages and totalItems excludes academic-only audit volume.
cases.expected: Pagination metadata is consistent within ITAdmin technical audit scope: totalItems reflects technical log count; pages are non-overlapping; academic mutation details are excluded per API §4.7 and permissions matrix AuditLog.read.

cases.title: ITAdmin denied academic attendance mutations, exports, and session control
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
Closed session with enrolled student and Absent AttendanceRecord exists.
Scheduled session exists; attendance report data spans institution scope.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Excused and reason as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and term-wide filters as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/open as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken as ITAdmin.
Inspect each response for HTTP 403 Forbidden and verify AttendanceRecord, session state, and export jobs are unchanged.
cases.expected: All academic mutation endpoints return HTTP 403 Forbidden per permissions matrix §3.2 and §5.5; attendance status, session state, and export queue remain unchanged; no partial export artifact is returned per BR-19.

cases.title: ITAdmin denied institution-wide attendance report and export without elevated academic grant
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
Attendance data exists across institution scope for active term.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with term-wide csv filters as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/terms with new term payload as ITAdmin.
Call PATCH /v1/policies/{policyId} with modified GPS radius as ITAdmin.
Inspect HTTP status, error.code, and response body for restricted data leakage.
cases.expected: Report, export, term creation, and policy mutation requests return HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null; no partial attendance rows, student identifiers, or cross-scope totals are disclosed per AC-16, AC-23, and BR-19.

cases.title: ITAdmin academic audit query without explicit grant returns denial or technical-scope-only payload
preconditions: Committed attendance mutation audit exists for known student-session pair with oldStatus, newStatus, and reason.
ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
AcademicAdmin token is available as authorized control.
steps: ITAdmin calls GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=attendance_status_mutation&targetId={studentSessionTarget}.
Parse response for HTTP 403 or filtered technical-scope-only items without academic PII.
Verify response contains no oldStatus, newStatus, student identifiers, or reason text from academic mutation.
AcademicAdmin calls same query as control and validates full FR-29 audit fields.
Confirm ITAdmin query produces no attendance or audit mutations as side effect.
cases.expected: Academic mutation audit query short-circuits with HTTP 403 or returns technical-scope-only entries without academic mutation payload per API §4.7 and permissions matrix §5.5 exception rule; AcademicAdmin control returns full audit evidence.

cases.title: ITAdmin browser journey reviews PG-15 operational audit logs with technical filter scope
preconditions: ITAdmin is logged in on desktop browser.
PG-15 /audit/logs is reachable with TableToolbar, DataTable, and AuditEntryRow components.
Fixture provides technical audit entries (login_lock, system_config) within ITAdmin operational scope.
steps: Navigate to /audit/logs and confirm audit log table loads with default timestamp desc sort.
Apply actionType filter for technical events (login_lock, system_config); verify table updates with scoped technical results.
Set from/to date range filters; confirm URL query params sync with toolbar state per PG-15 listing spec.
Navigate to page 2 via pagination controls if totalPages > 1.
Verify academic mutation edit controls are absent; AuditEntryRow displays operational fields read-only without attendance correction affordances.
cases.expected: PG-15 listing per FLOW-13 supports ITAdmin operational log review with filter, sort, and pagination; technical audit entries render read-only; academic write controls are hidden per FR-33, NFR-16, and NFR-UI-03.

cases.title: ITAdmin browser journey authenticates via PG-01 and confirms academic admin routes are gated
preconditions: ITAdmin credentials are provisioned for institution-issued login.
PG-01 /login is reachable; PG-13 /reports/attendance and PG-10 enrollment import routes exist.
Attendance data and class sections exist for gating verification.
steps: Navigate to /login, submit ITAdmin credentials via FRM-01, and confirm successful authentication redirect.
Verify post-login navigation exposes platform operations routes (audit logs) and hides or denies academic setup write routes (terms, policies, enrollment import).
Attempt direct URL navigation to /reports/attendance and observe access-denied or empty scoped state without institution-wide rows.
Attempt direct URL navigation to /admin/class-sections/{id}/enrollments import view and observe access denial.
Confirm PG-01 locked/throttled state messaging is available for failed login attempts per FR-36 security policy.
cases.expected: FLOW-14 staff authentication succeeds for ITAdmin; academic listing and import routes show access denial or hidden write controls without cross-scope data per FR-33, FR-36, and AC-23; login form displays neutral throttle messaging on repeated failures.
